summary: Typosquat of @solana/web3.js exfiltrating host data via postinstall script
details: This package mimics the legitimate @solana/web3.js library under the unrelated @solana-labs scope. package.json declares `"postinstall": "node install.js"`, which automatically runs install.js on `npm install`. install.js collects host identifiers (hostname appears in multiple reads) and exfiltrates them via an outbound network call (curl + POST). On any default install, the package executes attacker-controlled code that beacons installer host information to an external endpoint. The combination of (a) name confusion against the widely-used @solana/web3.js, (b) a postinstall lifecycle hook invoking a Node script, and (c) that script performing host-identifier collection plus outbound HTTP POST is a canonical install-time exfiltration shape.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
